channel.title: Sainik School Admission 2027 | AISSEE Guide & School Directory on Sainik School Eligibility Criteria 2026-27 for Class 6th & 9th
channel.description: Recent content in Sainik School Admission 2027 | AISSEE Guide & School Directory on Sainik School Eligibility Criteria 2026-27 for Class 6th & 9th

item.title: About Us | Sainik School East Siang Guide Team
item.description:
About Sainik School East Siang Guide

Sainik School East Siang Guide (sainikschooleastsiang.in) is a dedicated information portal that provides comprehensive, accurate, and up-to-date information about the All India Sainik School Entrance Exam (AISSEE) and all 33+ Sainik Schools in India. Our mission is to help every aspiring student and their parents navigate the Sainik School admission process with confidence.

Our Mission

We believe that every child deserves access to quality information about Sainik School admissions. Our goal is to:

item.title: Contact Us | Sainik School East Siang Guide
item.description:
Contact Us

Have questions about AISSEE 2027, Sainik School admissions, or anything related to the Sainik School system in India? We are here to help. Reach out to the Sainik School East Siang Guide team through any of the channels listed below.

Important: We are an independent information portal and cannot process official admission applications or provide official counselling. For official AISSEE queries, please visit exams.nta.ac.in/AISSEE or contact your preferred Sainik School directly.

item.title: Disclaimer | Sainik School East Siang Guide
item.description:
Disclaimer

Last Updated: 9 July 2026

Please read this disclaimer carefully before using the Sainik School East Siang Guide website (sainikschooleastsiang.in). By accessing and using this website, you acknowledge and agree to the terms outlined in this disclaimer.

1. General Information Purpose

The content published on sainikschooleastsiang.in is provided for general informational and educational purposes only. While we make every effort to ensure the accuracy and timeliness of the information presented, we make no warranties or representations of any kind, express or implied, about the completeness, accuracy, reliability, suitability, or availability of the website or the information, products, services, or related graphics contained on the website.

item.title: Page Not Found | Sainik School East Siang Guide
item.description:
404 — Page Not Found

Sorry, the page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved. Don’t worry — you can find everything you need about Sainik Schools and AISSEE right here.

Popular Pages

Here are the most visited sections of our website:

  • AISSEE 2027 Complete Guide — Everything about the All India Sainik School Entrance Exam: eligibility, syllabus, exam pattern, preparation tips, and more
  • Sainik Schools Directory — Complete list of all 33+ Sainik Schools in India with state-wise details, fee structures, and admission information
  • Blog & Latest Updates — Latest news, analysis, and tips about AISSEE and Sainik School admissions
  • About Us — Learn about our team and mission
  • Contact Us — Reach out to us for queries and feedback

What Were You Looking For?

AISSEE 2027 Information

If you are looking for AISSEE 2027 exam details, eligibility, or application process, visit our complete AISSEE guide.

item.title: Privacy Policy | Sainik School East Siang Guide
item.description:
Privacy Policy

Last Updated: 9 July 2026

Sainik School East Siang Guide (sainikschooleastsiang.in) is committed to protecting your privacy. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect information when you visit our website.

1. Information We Collect

1.1 Information You Provide Voluntarily

When you contact us through email, phone, WhatsApp, or social media, you may provide:

  • Name — To address you in our responses
  • Email address — To respond to your queries
  • Phone number — If you contact us via phone or WhatsApp
  • Query details — The questions or information you share with us

1.2 Information Collected Automatically

When you visit our website, certain information is collected automatically:

item.title: Sainik School Amaravathinagar – Tamil Nadu's Defence School
item.description: Key Takeaway: Sainik School Amaravathinagar, established in 1962, is located at the foothills of the Western Ghats in Udumalpet, Tirupur District, Tamil Nadu. Spread across 206 acres, the school is affiliated with the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) and operates under the Sainik Schools Society, Ministry of Defence, Government of India. Known as “Sainik School Madras” in its early years, it celebrated its Diamond Jubilee in 2022, marking sixty years of producing leaders for the Indian Armed Forces. The school is commonly known by its alumni community name — the Amaravians.

item.title: Sainik School Bhubaneswar – Odisha's Premier NDA School
item.description: Key Takeaway: Sainik School Bhubaneswar was started on 15 January 1962 by the late Shri Biju Patnaik, then Chief Minister of Odisha, and was inaugurated at its permanent campus by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru on 8 January 1964. Located in Bhubaneswar, the capital city of Odisha, the school is affiliated with the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) and operates as a co-educational residential school under the Sainik Schools Society, Ministry of Defence, Government of India. The school prepares cadets for entry into the National Defence Academy (NDA) and other defence institutions.

item.title: Sainik School Chittorgarh – Rajasthan's Premier Defence School
item.description: Key Takeaway: Sainik School Chittorgarh, established on 7 August 1961, is one of the earliest Sainik Schools in India. Spread across a sprawling 272-acre campus on Bhilwara Road in the historic city of Chittorgarh, Rajasthan, it holds the distinction of having 11 boarding houses — the most of any Sainik School in the country. The school features specialised facilities including an archery range, a shooting range, and an equitation centre, alongside a strong academic and NDA preparation programme.

item.title: Sainik School Ghorakhal – Uttarakhand's Hill Station School
item.description: Key Takeaway: Sainik School Ghorakhal was founded on 21 March 1966 on the magnificent estate of the Nawab of Rampur, near Nainital in Uttarakhand. The name “Ghorakhal” originates from an incident during the 1857 uprising when a British General’s horse died while drinking water from a pond in the area — “Ghora” meaning horse and “Khal” meaning pond. Spread across 500 acres, the school is one of the largest Sainik School campuses in India. It is affiliated with the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) and operates under the Sainik Schools Society, Ministry of Defence, Government of India.

item.title: Sainik School Kapurthala – Punjab's Historic Boarding School
item.description: Key Takeaway: Sainik School Kapurthala, inaugurated on 8 July 1961, is the seventh Sainik School established in India. Housed inside the magnificent Jagatjit Palace — a French Renaissance–style building modelled on the Palace of Versailles — it sits on a 245-acre campus in Kapurthala, Punjab. The school is affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE Affiliation No. 1680001) and operates under the Sainik Schools Society, Ministry of Defence, Government of India.

item.title: 5 Mistakes Parents Make During Sainik School Admission 2027
item.description:
5 Mistakes That Cost Parents Sainik School Admission Every Year

Thousands of students miss Sainik School admission each year. Not because they lack talent. Because their parents made avoidable mistakes during the application and preparation process.

Here are the 5 most common ones, based on 8 years of defense education consulting.

Mistake 1: Wrong Age Calculation

This is the single most common reason for application rejection.

The Problem

Parents calculate age from the date of birth to the exam date. AISSEE does not work that way. Age is counted from April 1 of the admission year, not the exam date.

item.title: Aamir - Defense Education Expert
item.description:
About Aamir

Aamir has been working in defense education for over 8 years. He started as a career counselor at a coaching center in Shillong, where he noticed that most parents had no clear understanding of how Sainik Schools actually work, what the admission process involves, or what career paths open up after graduation.

That gap in accessible, honest information is what led him to Sainik School Guide India.

Background

Aamir grew up in East Siang, Arunachal Pradesh. He attended a residential school himself, so he understands the adjustment students go through when they leave home for the first time. He studied political science at Delhi University and completed his counseling certification from the National Institute of Career Services.

item.title: AISSEE Previous Year Question Papers: Download PDF & Analysis
item.description:
Previous Year Papers: The Best Free Preparation Tool

Every topper will tell you the same thing: solving previous year papers is the most effective way to prepare for AISSEE. Not mock tests from coaching apps. Not random practice sheets. Actual papers that NTA set in previous years.

The reason is simple. AISSEE repeats patterns. The same type of questions appear every year. If you solve the last 5 years of papers, you will see the patterns yourself.

item.title: How to Fill AISSEE Application Form 2027: Step-by-Step Guide with Common Mistakes
item.description:
AISSEE 2027 Application Form: Exact Steps, Common Mistakes, Deadlines

Har saal hazaron students ka AISSEE form reject ho jaata hai. Photo galat size ka, documents missing, ya fee payment nahi hui. Yeh sab avoid ki ja sakti hai agar aapko pata ho ki exactly kya karna hai.

Yeh guide step-by-step batayega ki form kaise bharna hai, kaunse documents chahiye, aur kya galtiyan aapko avoid karni chahiye.

Important Dates

Event Date (Tentative)
Application Start October 2026
Last Date to Apply November 2026
Last Date for Fee Payment November 2026
Correction Window November 2026 (2-3 days)
Admit Card Release December 2026 / January 2027
Exam Date January 2027

Dates har saal change hoti hain. NTA website regularly check karo: exams.nta.nic.in

item.title: NDA After Sainik School: Complete Career Path from Sainik School to Indian Armed Forces
item.description:
Sainik School to NDA: The 12-Year Path to Becoming an Officer

Sainik School students have a 30-40% NDA selection rate. The national average is 5-7%. That gap exists because Sainik Schools start military preparation from Class 6, not after graduation.

The path from Class 6 admission to a commissioned officer takes about 12 years. Here is the timeline, the exam details, and the real numbers on what it takes.

Complete Timeline: Class 6 to Commissioned Officer

Year Age Class Milestone
Year 1 10-11 Class 6 Sainik School admission (AISSEE)
Year 2 11-12 Class 7 Foundation building, NCC starts
Year 3 12-13 Class 8 Sports & academics strengthen
Year 4 13-14 Class 9 Serious preparation begins
Year 5 14-15 Class 10 Board exam + NDA awareness
Year 6 15-16 Class 11 Stream selection (Science preferred)
Year 7 16-17 Class 12 Board exam + NDA written exam
Year 8 17-18 NDA Join National Defence Academy, Pune
Year 9-11 18-21 IMA/INA/AFA Service academy training
Year 12+ 21+ Officer Commissioned as Lieutenant/Equivalent

Total Duration: 12 years from Class 6 to commissioned officer.

item.title: Nisha - Education Content Specialist
item.description:
About Nisha

Nisha has been writing about education for over 5 years, with a focus on entrance exams and school admissions. She joined Sainik School Guide India because she saw how confusing the AISSEE process was for parents, especially those from rural areas or first-generation school families.

Before this, she worked as a content writer for an ed-tech company in Jaipur, where she created study material for competitive exams. That experience taught her how to explain complex topics in simple language, which is what she does now for Sainik School content.

item.title: Rifaul Hasan - Student Counselor
item.description:
About Rifaul Hasan

Rifaul Hasan has spent 7 years working with students in residential school settings. He understands the unique pressures that come with living away from home at a young age, dealing with strict routines, and competing with peers in both academics and sports.

He joined Sainik School Guide India because he noticed that most online content about Sainik Schools focused on admissions and exams, but almost nobody talked about what happens after a student gets in. The adjustment period, homesickness, peer pressure, and academic stress are real issues that families need to prepare for.

item.title: Sainik School Age Eligibility Calculator 2027 - Check Class 6 & Class 9 Age Limit
item.description:
Sainik School Age Calculator: Check If Your Child Is Eligible in 30 Seconds

4 lakh students apply for AISSEE every year. But thousands of applications get rejected because the child does not meet the age criteria. Parents fill the form, pay the fee, and prepare for months, only to find out at the document verification stage that their child is a few days too old or too young.

This calculator tells you instantly whether your child is eligible for Sainik School admission 2027. Enter the date of birth, select the admission year, and get a clear answer with no confusion.

item.title: Sainik School Ambikapur Chhattisgarh - Admission, Fees & Contact Details
item.description:
Sainik School Ambikapur — Chhattisgarh’s Military School in the Tribal Heartland

Chhattisgarh is one of India’s youngest states, carved out of Madhya Pradesh in 2000. Its northern region — the Surguja division — is tribal-dominated, forested, and economically underdeveloped. It is not the obvious location for a military school. Yet Sainik School Ambikapur, established in 2008, was deliberately placed here to serve the youth of Chhattisgarh’s tribal and rural communities, offering them a path to the National Defence Academy that was previously inaccessible.

item.title: Sainik School Amethi UP - Admission Process, Fees & Facilities
item.description:
Sainik School Amethi — UP’s Newest Military School, Born in a Pandemic

Sainik School Amethi has the distinction of being one of the youngest Sainik Schools in India — and the only one whose first academic session began during a global pandemic. The Memorandum of Agreement between the Ministry of Defence and the Uttar Pradesh state government was signed on 30th April 2015, but it took five years for the school to become operational. On 15th July 2020, Sainik School Amethi commenced its first academic session — only to find that COVID-19 had shut down physical classrooms. The school resorted to online classes from 10th August 2020, making its founding story uniquely modern.

item.title: Sainik School Balachadi Jamnagar Gujarat - Complete Admission Guide 2026
item.description:
Sainik School Balachadi — Gujarat’s Coastal Military School with a WWII Secret

The golden sands of the Gulf of Kutch stretch behind the campus. The salt-laden wind from the Arabian Sea is a constant companion. And somewhere in the school’s history lies a story that most visitors don’t know: during World War II, this very site was a camp for Polish children — refugees from the Soviet deportations who found sanctuary in India through the extraordinary compassion of Jam Sahib Digvijaysinhji Ranjitsinhji of Nawanagar.

item.title: Sainik School Bijapur Karnataka - History, Admission & Fee Structure
item.description:
Sainik School Bijapur — Where Karnataka’s Dynastic Glory Meets Military Discipline

Bijapur — now officially Vijayapura — is a city defined by its sultanate. The Adil Shahi dynasty (1490–1686) left behind the Gol Gumbaz, the second-largest dome in the world, and a cityscape of mosques, mausoleums, and fortifications that speak to centuries of military architecture. It is entirely fitting, then, that Karnataka’s Sainik School was established here, on a 406-acre campus on Athani Road, where the Adil Shahi legacy of strategic thinking and martial tradition is channeled into preparing young cadets for the Indian Armed Forces.

item.title: Sainik School Chandrapur Maharashtra - Admission, Fees & Contact
item.description:
Sainik School Chandrapur — Vidarbha’s New Military Academy

Chandrapur district in eastern Maharashtra — known for its coal mines, tiger reserves, and scorching summers — is not the most obvious location for a military school. But Sainik School Chandrapur, established as part of the Government of India’s expansion of the Sainik Schools network, was placed here to serve the youth of Vidarbha — Maharashtra’s eastern region that has historically lagged behind western Maharashtra in educational infrastructure.

item.title: Sainik School Chhingchhip Mizoram - Admission Process & Fee Details
item.description:
Sainik School Chhingchhip — Where Girls Made History Before the Nation Caught Up

On 4th June 2018, six girls walked through the gates of Sainik School Chhingchhip in Mizoram and made national headlines. “History was created on June 4 when six girls joined the Sainik School Chhingchhip in Mizoram,” reported the Times of India. This was three years before the Government of India officially directed all Sainik Schools to admit girls in 2021. Mizoram’s Sainik School was already there.

item.title: Sainik School Counselling 2027: E-Counselling Process, Spot Round & Seat Allotment
item.description:
Counselling is Where You Actually Get the Seat. Don’t Mess It Up.

Clearing AISSEE is only half the battle. The other half is counselling, where seats are allocated based on your rank, category, and school preferences. Many students who score well lose their seat because they filled choices incorrectly or missed a deadline.

This guide covers the entire counselling process step by step so you don’t make those mistakes.

How Sainik School Counselling Works

After AISSEE results are declared, qualified candidates must register on the AISSAC portal for e-counselling. The process has multiple rounds.

item.title: Sainik School East Siang Arunachal Pradesh - Admission & Fee Guide 2026
item.description:
Sainik School East Siang — Gateway to NDA from India’s Eastern Frontier

Arunachal Pradesh is where India wakes up first. The state’s easternmost districts see the sun before anyone else in the country. It is also one of India’s most strategically sensitive regions — sharing a disputed border with China along the Line of Actual Control. The establishment of Sainik School East Siang on 27th August 2018, as the 28th Sainik School of India, was a deliberate move to build military awareness and officer recruitment from this frontier state.

item.title: Sainik School Goalpara Assam - Admission, Fees & NDA Track Record
item.description:
Sainik School Goalpara — Northeast India’s Gateway to the Uniform

The Brahmaputra flows wide and slow through Assam, and on its southern bank, in the district town of Goalpara, sits a school that has been preparing the sons and daughters of the Northeast for military service since 1962. Sainik School Goalpara is not just another boarding school — it is the only Sainik School in Assam, and one of only two in the entire Northeast (the other being Sainik School Punglwa in Nagaland). For cadets from Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, and Nagaland, Goalpara is the nearest path to the National Defence Academy.

item.title: Sainik School Gopalganj Bihar - Admission Process, Fees & Facilities
item.description:
Sainik School Gopalganj — Bihar’s Second Military School in the Land of Warriors

Bihar has a military recruitment tradition that few states can match. The Bihar Regiment, one of the Indian Army’s most decorated infantry regiments, draws from the state’s warrior communities — Rajputs, Bhumihars, Yadavs, and others who have served in every major conflict since independence. Sainik School Gopalganj, established on 12th October 2003 in Sipaya village near Gopalganj, was created to serve this martial heartland.

item.title: Sainik School Imphal Manipur - History, Admission & Fee Structure
item.description:
Sainik School Imphal — From the WWII Battlefield to the NDA Pipeline

Imphal has seen war up close. In 1944, the Japanese Imperial Army launched Operation U-Go, pushing through the jungles of Burma to capture Imphal and Kohima — the two battles that Winston Churchill called “the forgotten battles” but military historians regard as the “Stalingrad of the East.” The battles of Imphal and Kohima broke the Japanese advance into India and turned the tide of the war in Southeast Asia.

item.title: Sainik School Jhansi UP - Admission, Fees & Contact Details
item.description:
Sainik School Jhansi — From Rani Lakshmibai’s Fort to the NDA Pipeline

Jhansi is synonymous with one name: Rani Lakshmibai. The warrior queen who led the 1857 Rebellion against the British East India Company, fighting to the death at the age of 29, made Jhansi a symbol of Indian resistance. It is entirely fitting, then, that Sainik School Jhansi — established in 2019 — was placed in this city of defiance.

item.title: Sainik School Jhunjhunu Rajasthan - Admission Process & Fee Guide
item.description:
Sainik School Jhunjhunu — Shekhawati’s New Military Academy

Jhunjhunu district in Rajasthan’s Shekhawati region has a claim that no other district in India can match: it sends more soldiers per capita to the Indian Army than any other district in the country. The Shekhawati Rajputs — a warrior clan that traces its lineage to Rao Shekha (1433-1488) — have served in the Indian Army since the colonial era. The Jat and Rajput communities of this semi-arid region have a military service rate that dwarfs even Punjab and Haryana.

item.title: Sainik School Kalikiri Andhra Pradesh - Admission, Fees & Facilities
item.description:
Sainik School Kalikiri — Andhra Pradesh’s Second Sainik School in the Shadow of Tirumala

Sainik School Kalikiri sits in Chittoor district, Andhra Pradesh — the district that houses Tirumala, the world’s most visited Hindu temple. The spiritual energy of the Tirumala hills is visible from the school campus, but the institution itself is focused on a very different kind of preparation: grooming young cadets for the National Defence Academy.

Established in 2015, Sainik School Kalikiri is Andhra Pradesh’s second Sainik School — the first being Sainik School Korukonda (est. 1962). The school was created to serve southern Andhra Pradesh and the Rayalaseema region, which has a strong military recruitment tradition but previously had no Sainik School within reasonable distance.

item.title: Sainik School Kazhakootam Kerala - Admission, Fees & Campus Life
item.description:
Sainik School Kazhakootam — Kerala’s Military School by the Arabian Sea

Kerala does not have a strong military recruitment tradition. The state’s economy runs on remittances from the Gulf, IT services, and tourism — not on military service. Yet Sainik School Kazhakootam, established in 1962 on a 225-acre campus just 18 km from Thiruvananthapuram, has been quietly producing officers for the Indian Armed Forces for over six decades, defying the state’s civilian orientation.

item.title: Sainik School Kodagu Karnataka - Admission Process & Fee Structure
item.description:
Sainik School Kodagu — Where Coffee Country Meets Military Discipline

Kodagu (Coorg) is Karnataka’s highland jewel — a district of rolling hills, coffee plantations, and mist-covered forests that produce some of India’s finest arabica beans. But Kodagu has another, less-celebrated tradition: military service. The Kodava community — the indigenous people of Coorg — have served in the Indian Army in disproportionate numbers for their population size, producing generals, colonels, and decorated soldiers.

item.title: Sainik School Korukonda Vizianagaram - Admission, Fees & NDA Selections
item.description:
Sainik School Korukonda — Andhra Pradesh’s Powerhouse of NDA Selections

In 2021, Sainik School Korukonda received 11,000 applications for just 80 seats in Class VI. That is a selection ratio of roughly 1:137 — more competitive than many IIT entrance exams. The reason is simple: Korukonda has one of the highest NDA selection rates among all Sainik Schools, and parents across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana know it.

Established in 1962, Sainik School Korukonda is located in Vizianagaram district of Andhra Pradesh. It was one of the early Sainik Schools created under the Government of India’s initiative to develop a pipeline of officer material for the National Defence Academy. Over six decades, the school has built a reputation for academic rigor, physical toughness, and a singular focus on producing commissioned officers.

item.title: Sainik School Kunjpura Karnal Haryana - India's Oldest Sainik School
item.description:
Sainik School Kunjpura — Haryana’s First Five

When the Government of India established the first Sainik Schools in 1961, only five were in the inaugural batch. Sainik School Kunjpura, in Karnal district of Haryana, was one of them. The others were Satara (Maharashtra), Kunjpura (Haryana), Amaravathinagar (Tamil Nadu), Korukonda (Andhra Pradesh), and Chittorgarh (Rajasthan). That Kunjpura made the first cut is no accident — Haryana, with its deep military tradition and the highest per-capita representation in the Indian Army (alongside Punjab and Rajasthan), was a natural choice.

item.title: Sainik School Lucknow UP - Admission, Fees & Contact Information
item.description:
Sainik School Lucknow — The Nawab’s City Produces Officers

Lucknow is a city of contradictions. The capital of Uttar Pradesh is known for its tehzeeb (etiquette), its kebabs, its Nawabi culture, and the ruins of the Residency — the site of the 1857 Siege of Lucknow, one of the most dramatic episodes of the Indian Rebellion. It is also a city with a strong military presence: the Central Command of the Indian Army is headquartered here, and the city has produced officers for the armed forces across generations.

item.title: Sainik School Mainpuri UP - Admission Process, Fees & Facilities
item.description:
Sainik School Mainpuri — Western UP’s Military Academy

Mainpuri district in western Uttar Pradesh is agricultural heartland — flat, fertile, and deeply connected to the land. The region’s Jat and Yadav communities have a strong tradition of military service, with many families having multiple generations in the Indian Army. Sainik School Mainpuri, established in 2019, was created to serve this community, offering structured NDA preparation to a region that was previously underserved by the Sainik Schools network.

item.title: Sainik School Mansasbal Jammu Kashmir - Admission & Fee Details
item.description:
Sainik School Mansasbal — The Kashmir Valley’s First Sainik School

The Kashmir Valley — paradise on earth, conflict zone, and one of the most beautiful places on the planet — has never had a Sainik School. While Jammu had Sainik School Nagrota (est. 1970), the Valley’s youth had no military-oriented school within their region. Sainik School Mansasbal, established in 2023, changed that.

Located near the Mansasbal Lake in the Ganderbal district of central Kashmir, the school is positioned in one of the Valley’s most scenic locations. Mansasbal Lake — sometimes called the “supreme gem of all Kashmir lakes” — provides a stunning backdrop to a school dedicated to preparing Kashmir’s youth for military service.

item.title: Sainik School Nagrota Jammu - History, Admission & Fee Structure
item.description:
Sainik School Nagrota — Defending the Frontline from the Classroom

Nagrota sits on the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway, a garrison town that has seen more than its share of military action. In 2016, a terrorist attack on an army camp here killed seven soldiers. In 2020, another attack was foiled. The town is not a tourist destination — it is a frontline community where military preparedness is not an abstraction but a daily reality.

item.title: Sainik School Nalanda Bihar - Admission, Fees & Academic Results
item.description:
Sainik School Nalanda — Where India’s Oldest University Meets Its Newest Officers

Nalanda is where India’s intellectual tradition began. The ancient Nalanda University — founded in the 5th century CE, destroyed by Bakhtiyar Khilji in 1193 — was the world’s first residential university, attracting scholars from China, Korea, Japan, Tibet, Mongolia, Turkey, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia. Today, a modern Nalanda University has been re-established nearby, and Sainik School Nalanda, established in 2003, carries forward the district’s educational legacy in a different direction: preparing officers for the Indian Armed Forces.

item.title: Sainik School Punglwa Nagaland - Admission Process & Fee Guide
item.description:
Sainik School Punglwa — Nagaland’s Military School on the Edge of the Frontier

Punglwa sits in the Peren district of Nagaland, close to the Myanmar border. This is Naga country — the land of the Konyak, Ao, Angami, and other Naga tribes who were once feared headhunters and are now among India’s most decorated soldiers. The Assam Rifles, in particular, has deep ties to the Naga communities, and many Naga youth serve in the armed forces.

item.title: Sainik School Purulia West Bengal - Admission, Fees & NDA Results
item.description:
Sainik School Purulia — Knowledge Through Discipline in Bengal’s Red Soil

Purulia is not Bengal’s poster child. While Kolkata basks in literary glory and Darjeeling in Himalayan beauty, Purulia sits in the westernmost corner of West Bengal — a district of red soil, sal forests, and tribal communities that the rest of the state often forgets. But Purulia has produced something that Kolkata hasn’t: a Sainik School, established in 1962, that has been grooming officers for the Indian Armed Forces from this overlooked corner for over six decades.

item.title: Sainik School Rewa Madhya Pradesh - Admission, Fees & Campus Details
item.description:
Sainik School Rewa — From the Land of White Tigers to the Ranks of the Armed Forces

Rewa is known for one extraordinary claim to fame: in 1951, a white tiger named Mohan was captured in the forests near Rewa by Maharaja Martand Singh. That white tiger lineage — the only one in captivity — put this quiet Vindhyan city on the global map. A decade later, in 1962, the Government of India added another distinction: a Sainik School, established on 242.77 acres of land allotted by the Madhya Pradesh state government in the Civil Lines area.

item.title: Sainik School Rewari Haryana - Admission Process & Fee Structure
item.description:
Sainik School Rewari — Ahirwal’s Gateway to the Officer Corps

Rewari district in southern Haryana is the heart of Ahirwal — the land of the Ahir (Yadav) community. The Ahirs have a martial tradition that is deeply embedded in their culture: the Bhagavad Gita’s warrior-sage Arjuna is claimed as an ancestor, and the community has served in the Indian Army through the Kumaon Regiment, the Rajputana Rifles, and other units. Ahirwal’s contribution to military service is disproportionate to its population — a fact that makes Sainik School Rewari, established in 2008, a natural fit.

item.title: Sainik School Sambalpur Odisha - Admission, Fees & Contact
item.description:
Sainik School Sambalpur — Western Odisha’s New Military Academy

Sambalpur is the cultural capital of western Odisha — a region defined by the Mahanadi River, the Hirakud Dam, and the Sambalpuri textile tradition. It is also a region with a significant tribal population and limited educational infrastructure compared to coastal Odisha. Sainik School Sambalpur, established as Odisha’s second Sainik School (after Bhubaneswar), was created to serve western Odisha’s youth, offering them a path to the National Defence Academy that was previously inaccessible.

item.title: Sainik School Satara Maharashtra - History, Fees & NDA Track Record
item.description:
Sainik School Satara — Where It All Began

Every chain has a first link. For India’s Sainik Schools, that link is Satara.

On 23rd June 1961, Sainik School Satara opened its doors — the very first Sainik School in India. While 32 more would follow across the country, Satara holds the distinction of being the pioneer, the proof of concept, the school that demonstrated the Sainik School model could work. Established by the Government of India under the vision of Defence Minister V.K. Krishna Menon, Satara was the answer to a question that had haunted India’s military establishment: how do you build a pipeline of officer-quality recruits from the civilian population?

item.title: Sainik School Scholarship 2027: Complete Fee Concession Guide for SC/ST, Defence & Meritorious Students
item.description:
Sainik School Fees: ₹2.2 Lakh. But 60% Students Pay Less.

Sainik School ki annual fees ₹2,00,000 - ₹2,50,000 hai. Lekin yeh baat bahut kam logon ko pata hai — 60% se zyada students ko kisi na kisi form mein fee concession milta hai. SC/ST, Defence, aur meritorious students ke liye fees aadhi se bhi kam ho jaati hai.

Yeh guide batayegi kaun si scholarship hai, kaise apply karna hai, aur kitni fees bach sakti hai.

item.title: Sainik School Sujanpur Tira HP - Admission, Fees & Facilities
item.description:
Sainik School Sujanpur Tira — Himachal Pradesh’s Hill Station Military Academy

Sujanpur Tira is a small town in the Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, known for its ancient fort, its Holi fair, and its position on the banks of the Beas River. But for the cadets of Sainik School Sujanpur Tira, established in 1978, the town represents something more significant: the starting point of a journey to the National Defence Academy.

item.title: Sainik School Tilaiya Jharkhand - Forward Ever, Fees & NDA Results
item.description:
Sainik School Tilaiya — Forward Ever, Backward Never

The motto says it all: Forward Ever. Sainik School Tilaiya, established on 16th September 1963, is the 12th Sainik School in India’s chain of 33. Located at Tilaiya Dam in Koderma district of Jharkhand (formerly Bihar), the school sits in the Chotanagpur Plateau — a landscape of laterite soil, sal forests, and rocky terrain that has produced some of India’s toughest soldiers.

item.title: Sainik School vs Military School: Which is Better for Your Child?
item.description:
Sainik School vs Military School: The Data-Driven Comparison

Sainik Schools charge ₹2-2.5 lakh per year. Military Schools charge ₹1-1.5 lakh. Sainik Schools have 33+ campuses. Military Schools have 5. Sainik Schools allow girls. Military Schools do not.

Those are the headline differences. Here is the full breakdown so you can decide which one fits your family.

Quick Comparison Table

Feature Sainik School Military School (RMS)
Total Schools 33+ 5
Conducting Body Sainik Schools Society Ministry of Defence
Entrance Exam AISSEE RMS CET
Classes 6 to 12 6 to 12
Gender Boys & Girls Boys only
Fee (Annual) ₹2-2.5 Lakh ₹1-1.5 Lakh
Focus NDA preparation Overall military training
Affiliation CBSE CBSE

Source: Sainik Schools Society and Ministry of Defence official data

item.title: Sainik School vs RIMC vs Rashtriya Military School: Complete Comparison 2027
item.description:
Three Schools, One Goal: Which One Fits Your Child?

RIMC, Sainik School, and Rashtriya Military School (RMS) all prepare students for NDA. But they are very different in terms of admission difficulty, fees, eligibility, and the kind of student they suit best.

This comparison is based on real data, not marketing claims. Read it before you decide where to apply.

Quick Comparison Table

Feature RIMC Sainik School RMS
Full Name Rashtriya Indian Military College Sainik Schools (33+) Rashtriya Military Schools (5)
Established 1922 1961 onwards 1925 onwards
Location Dehradun only 33+ schools across India Ajmer, Bangalore, Belgaum, Chail, Dholpur
Classes 8 to 12 6 to 12 6 to 12
Gender Boys only Boys + Girls (since 2021) Boys + Girls
Board CBSE CBSE CBSE
Run By Ministry of Defence Sainik Schools Society Ministry of Defence
Annual Fee ₹70,000-80,000 ₹2,00,000-2,50,000 ₹50,000-1,00,000
Entrance Exam RIMC Entrance (twice a year) AISSEE (once a year, by NTA) RMS CET (once a year)
Exam Difficulty Hardest Moderate Moderate-High
NDA Selection Rate Very High High (25-30% of NDA cadets) Very High
Coaching Needed Yes (English level is very high) Optional Optional

Exam Difficulty: Which is Toughest?

Aspect RIMC AISSEE (Sainik School) RMS CET
Maths Level Very High (Class 7-8 level) Moderate (NCERT level) Moderate-High
English Level Very High (essay, comprehension) Moderate Moderate
GK/Science High Moderate Moderate
Interview Yes (carries real weight) No No
Total Applicants ~15,000 ~4,00,000 ~50,000
Selection Rate ~2-3% ~2-3% ~3-5%

RIMC is the hardest exam. The English standard is very high, and the interview round eliminates many candidates. AISSEE has the most competition (4 lakh applicants) but the paper is more predictable. RMS sits in between.

item.title: Sainik Schools for Goa Students - Complete List 2026
item.description:
Sainik Schools for Goa Students

Goa does not have its own Sainik School. Students from Goa apply to Sainik School Balachadi in Gujarat. All schools are CBSE-affiliated, fully residential, and admit students through AISSEE conducted by NTA.

Sainik Schools for Goa Students ke Sainik Schools

School Location Established Board
Sainik School Balachadi, Gujarat Balachadi, Gujarat Goa students apply here CBSE

Admission Process

Admission in all Sainik Schools is through AISSEE (All India Sainik School Entrance Exam) conducted by NTA.

item.title: Sainik Schools in Andhra Pradesh - Complete List 2026
item.description:

Andhra Pradesh has 2 Sainik Schools. Sainik School Korukonda is one of the oldest in India. All schools are CBSE-affiliated, fully residential, and admit students through AISSEE conducted by NTA.

Andhra Pradesh ke Sainik Schools

School Location Established Board
Sainik School Korukonda Korukonda 1962 CBSE
Sainik School Kalikiri Kalikiri 2019 CBSE

Admission in all Sainik Schools is through AISSEE (All India Sainik School Entrance Exam) conducted by NTA.

item.title: Sainik Schools in Arunachal Pradesh - Complete List 2026
item.description:
Sainik Schools in Arunachal Pradesh

Arunachal Pradesh has 1 Sainik School in East Siang district. All schools are CBSE-affiliated, fully residential, and admit students through AISSEE conducted by NTA.

Arunachal Pradesh ke Sainik Schools

School Location Established Board
Sainik School East Siang East Siang 2020 CBSE

Admission Process

Admission in all Sainik Schools is through AISSEE (All India Sainik School Entrance Exam) conducted by NTA.

  • Class 6: Age 10-12 years, currently in Class 5
  • Class 9: Age 13-15 years, currently in Class 8
  • Application: Online at exams.nta.nic.in
  • Exam: Usually held in January
  • 67% seats reserved for home state students

Fee Structure (Approximate)

Component Annual Fee
Tuition ₹85,000 - ₹90,000
Hostel ₹42,000 - ₹48,000
Mess ₹55,000 - ₹65,000
Total ₹2,00,000 - ₹2,50,000

SC/ST students get up to 50% fee concession. Defence personnel wards get 25% concession.

item.title: Sainik Schools in Punjab - Complete List 2026
item.description:
Sainik Schools in Punjab - Complete Guide 2026

Punjab ka Sainik School India ke sabse prestigious defence schools mein se ek hai. Punjab ki strong military tradition ke kaaran yahaan ke Sainik Schools mein hamesha se demand rahi hai. Yeh schools students ko NDA, Indian Army, Navy aur Air Force ke liye taiyaar karte hain.

Punjab ke Sainik School mein admission AISSEE (All India Sainik Schools Entrance Exam) ke through hota hai jo NTA dwara conduct kiya jaata hai. Yahaan aapko Punjab ke Sainik School ki complete information milegi.

item.title: Sainik Schools in Rajasthan - Complete List 2026
item.description:
Sainik Schools in Rajasthan - Complete Guide 2026

Rajasthan mein Sainik Schools ka ek important role hai jo students ko armed forces ke liye taiyaar karta hai. Yeh schools NTA dwara conduct hone wale AISSEE (All India Sainik Schools Entrance Exam) ke through admission dete hain. Rajasthan ke Sainik Schools mein quality education ke saath defence training bhi di jaati hai.

Agar aap apne bacche ka admission Sainik School mein karana chahte hain, toh yeh guide aapke liye hai. Yahaan aapko Rajasthan ke sabhi Sainik Schools ki complete information milegi.

item.title: Sainik Schools in Tamil Nadu - Complete List 2026
item.description:
Sainik Schools in Tamil Nadu - Complete Guide 2026

Tamil Nadu mein Sainik Schools students ko armed forces ki taiyaari karne ka ek behtareen mauqa dete hain. Yeh schools CBSE board se affiliated hain aur AISSEE (All India Sainik Schools Entrance Exam) ke through admission dete hain. Tamil Nadu ke Sainik Schools academic excellence ke saath-saath physical fitness aur leadership skills bhi develop karte hain.

Tamil Nadu ke Sainik Schools mein admission lene ke liye AISEE entrance exam dena hota hai. Yahaan aapko Tamil Nadu ke sabhi Sainik Schools ki complete information milegi.

item.title: Sainik Schools in Uttarakhand - Complete List 2026
item.description:
Sainik Schools in Uttarakhand

Uttarakhand has 1 Sainik School in Nainital district. All schools are CBSE-affiliated, fully residential, and admit students through AISSEE conducted by NTA.

Uttarakhand ke Sainik Schools

School Location Established Board
Sainik School Ghorakhal Ghorakhal 1966 CBSE

Admission Process

Admission in all Sainik Schools is through AISSEE (All India Sainik School Entrance Exam) conducted by NTA.

  • Class 6: Age 10-12 years, currently in Class 5
  • Class 9: Age 13-15 years, currently in Class 8
  • Application: Online at exams.nta.nic.in
  • Exam: Usually held in January
  • 67% seats reserved for home state students

Fee Structure (Approximate)

Component Annual Fee
Tuition ₹85,000 - ₹90,000
Hostel ₹42,000 - ₹48,000
Mess ₹55,000 - ₹65,000
Total ₹2,00,000 - ₹2,50,000

SC/ST students get up to 50% fee concession. Defence personnel wards get 25% concession.

item.title: Sainik Schools in West Bengal - Complete List 2026
item.description:
Sainik Schools in West Bengal

West Bengal has 1 Sainik School in Purulia. All schools are CBSE-affiliated, fully residential, and admit students through AISSEE conducted by NTA.

West Bengal ke Sainik Schools

School Location Established Board
Sainik School Purulia Purulia 1962 CBSE

Admission Process

Admission in all Sainik Schools is through AISSEE (All India Sainik School Entrance Exam) conducted by NTA.

  • Class 6: Age 10-12 years, currently in Class 5
  • Class 9: Age 13-15 years, currently in Class 8
  • Application: Online at exams.nta.nic.in
  • Exam: Usually held in January
  • 67% seats reserved for home state students

Fee Structure (Approximate)

Component Annual Fee
Tuition ₹85,000 - ₹90,000
Hostel ₹42,000 - ₹48,000
Mess ₹55,000 - ₹65,000
Total ₹2,00,000 - ₹2,50,000

SC/ST students get up to 50% fee concession. Defence personnel wards get 25% concession.

item.title: Sameer - Education Researcher
item.description:
About Sameer

Sameer is an education researcher at Sainik School Guide India. He has spent over 6 years studying how students perform in competitive entrance exams like AISSEE, and what patterns separate successful candidates from the rest.

His background is in data analysis. Before joining Sainik School Guide, he worked with coaching institutes in Bihar and Uttar Pradesh, tracking student performance across multiple batches. That experience gave him a practical understanding of what works and what doesn’t in exam preparation.

item.title: Terms of Service - Sainik School Guide India
item.description:
Terms of Service

Last Updated: July 2026

By accessing and using sainikschooleastsiang.in (“the Website”), you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the Website.

About This Website

Sainik School Guide India is an independent educational resource. We are NOT affiliated with:

  • Ministry of Defence, Government of India
  • Sainik Schools Society
  • National Testing Agency (NTA)
  • Any individual Sainik School
  • National Defence Academy (NDA)

For official information, always refer to:
